description_long: This is a mid-rise apartment building with a rectangular footprint and a utilitarian, weathered concrete facade. The front features six stories of regularly spaced, dark-framed windows arranged in a grid, with the ground floor showing a rust-stained section that may indicate a former commercial or service area. The sides and rear of the building are plain, with no windows or architectural detailing, emphasizing a functional design. The building's exterior is predominantly gray, with streaks and discoloration suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The structure appears solid and robust, likely constructed from reinforced concrete and steel, with glass used for the windows. There are no visible balconies or decorative elements, and the entrance is implied to be at the base of the front facade.
description: A six-story, rectangular apartment building with a grid of dark windows on a weathered gray concrete facade. The structure is utilitarian, with no balconies and a rust-stained ground floor.
description_view_0: A plain, windowless concrete side of the building, showing vertical streaks and weathering.
description_view_1: The front facade with six stories of dark, grid-aligned windows and a rust-stained ground floor, indicating the main entrance.
description_view_2: Another plain, windowless concrete side, similar to view 0, with visible weathering.
description_view_3: The rear of the building, mostly featureless concrete with two dark rectangular sections at the base, possibly service entrances or vents.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in a symmetrical, evenly spaced grid pattern across the front facade, with each floor having a consistent row of windows aligned vertically and horizontally.
